text:
• Zack starts to make a recipe, but he gets distracted and puts in 3 bottles of glue and 1 teaspoon of baking soda.
    • What might he do to adjust the quantities so that what he has started with can still make slime? Explain your reasoning.

Since he needs 1 teaspoon of baking soda when using 2 bottles of glue, he needs 1/2 teaspoon of baking soda for every bottle of glue used.

For 3 bottles of glue, Zack would need 3 ⋅ 1/2 = 3/2 = 1.5 teaspoons of baking soda.

Or students might reason as follows:

1 teaspoon of baking soda for every 2 bottles of glue

↓ × 1/2 ↓ × 1/2

? teaspoon of baking soda for every 3 bottles of glue

1 1/2

Since he has already added 1 teaspoon of baking soda to his mixture, he would need to add (1.5 - 1 =) 0.5 teaspoons of baking soda.
